---
title: "journal"
author: "Hayden Simm"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

We are deciding to work in the tidyverse, so we loading in the data using "read_csv" 

possible things to investigate:
- look at how different tissues are activated with the different acclimation conditions
- look at acclimation and body condition 
- MANOVA 

Predictor variables Acclimation temperature, salinity, and assay temperature are factors. 
Body condition is continuous

Boxplot: plotted the assay temp by enzyme activity and body condition. Grouped the values by 
acclimation temperature. 

COX activity plot shows that the 10 degrees acclimated enzyme is has higher activity than the 24 degrees acclimated fish. Means the fish has 
higher capacity for oxygen consumption.. so because it was acclimated at this temperature it can perform better than the warm acclimated fish??
When in warmer temperatures COX activities are similar for the different temperature acclimated scenarios  

CS activity plot has 10 degree acclimated lower than than the 24 degrees acclimated for both test temperatures 

Few outliers in the body condition plot. 10 degrees acclimated have better body condition than the 24 degree acclimated. 

Logging the enzyme activites to get them on a comparable scale.
Doing RDA and MANCOVA analysis
